mnemonics: FLUORIDE: Found Locally Under Rocks, In Drinking-water Endangers enamel
keyPoints: Excess fluoride causes dental fluorosis (tooth mottling) and skeletal fluorosis (bone deformities)
Fluoride enters groundwater through dissolution of fluoride-containing minerals like fluorite
The WHO recommended limit for fluoride in drinking water is 1.5 mg/L
commonMistakes: Thinking all fluoride levels are harmful (some is beneficial for dental health)
Assuming boiling water removes fluoride (it actually concentrates it)
